item.title: Maharaj shines with multiple gold medals at Swimming Championship
Ball retention will be key - Kolinisau
item.description: Ball retention will be key as the Fiji Airways Men's National 7s team prepares for its final pool match and looks ahead to the Cup quarter-final at the World Championship in Valladolid, Spain. Kolinisau says numbers at the breakdown will be crucial as well, and making sure they have the right numbers at the breakdown is important.
item.content:encoded:
Ball retention will be key as the Fiji Airways Men's National 7s team prepares for its final pool match and looks ahead to the Cup quarter-final at the World Championship in Valladolid, Spain.

Kolinisau says numbers at the breakdown will be crucial as well, and making sure they have the right numbers at the breakdown is important.

He says players should not doubt themselves when executing decisions on the field, and having the right mindset will be vital as teams are competing on the mental aspect of the game.

Kolinisau reminded the players that discipline will be key, and the players should maintain their composure during the knockout stages.

Fiji Airways is through to the Cup quarterfinals and will next play hosts Spain in their last pool match at 12.12am tomorrow.

The Cup quarterfinals are at 2.27am tomorrow.

The Cup semi finals are at 8.44pm tomorrow before the Cup final kicks off at 2.26am Monday.

Meanwhile, the Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s side suffered a 26-19 defeat to the USA before being thrashed 45-5 by Australia in their opening two pool matches.

The Women's cup quarter finals are at 12.49am tomorrow.

The cup semifinals are at 8pm tomorrow, while the Women's cup final kicks off at 1.46am Monday.

item.title: Six records broken on opening day of National Schools Swimming Championships
item.description: Drasa Avenue School also etched its name into the record books after breaking the Girls 200 metres Medley Relay record with a time of 2 minutes 22.62 seconds, surpassing the previous mark of 2 minutes 25.16 seconds set by International School Nadi in 2019. Home-schooled swimmer Thaddeus Kwong also delivered a record-breaking performance in the 200 metres Freestyle, touching the wall in 2 minutes 2
item.content:encoded:
Drasa Avenue School also etched its name into the record books after breaking the Girls 200 metres Medley Relay record with a time of 2 minutes 22.62 seconds, surpassing the previous mark of 2 minutes 25.16 seconds set by International School Nadi in 2019.

Home-schooled swimmer Thaddeus Kwong also delivered a record-breaking performance in the 200 metres Freestyle, touching the wall in 2 minutes 2.77 seconds to break the previous championship record of 2 minutes 5.65 seconds set by Taichi Vakasama in 2017. The competition concludes today.

item.title: Stay true to your purpose with Drua - Togiatama
item.description: Swire Shipping Fijian Drua hooker Zuriel Togiatama marked a major milestone last night, making his 50th appearance for the franchise in their clash against the Queensland Reds. Speaking from Brisbane, Australia, Togiatama reflected on the achievement and shared a simple message with his teammates: stay true to your purpose.
item.content:encoded:
Swire Shipping Fijian Drua hooker Zuriel Togiatama marked a major milestone last night, making his 50th appearance for the franchise in their clash against the Queensland Reds.

Speaking from Brisbane, Australia, Togiatama reflected on the achievement and shared a simple message with his teammates: stay true to your purpose.

The dynamic hooker has been a symbol of loyalty, dedication, and hard work since joining the Drua as a young teenager determined to pursue his rugby dreams.

His commitment to the club has seen him grow into one of the franchise’s most dependable players.

Togiatama made his debut for the Drua during the team's inaugural Super Rugby Pacific season in 2022 and has since become an integral part of the squad.

As he celebrated the milestone, Togiatama expressed gratitude to his wife for being his constant source of strength throughout his rugby journey.

He also thanked Almighty God for blessing him with the opportunity to do what he loves both on and off the field, and acknowledged his parents and siblings for their unwavering support and encouragement.

The New Zealand-born player says to be part of a special club like the Drua is a blessing, and people don't get to see the full script the club does for its players.

The Queensland Reds beat the Drua 45-24 in their last match of the season.

In today's matches, the Brumbies take on Moana Pasifika at 4.35pm, the Chiefs face the Blues at 7.05pm, while the Force will take on the Warratahs at 9.35pm.

item.title: Fiji FACT: Defending champions Labasa pooled with Rewa, Suva and Navua
Messi to represent Argentina at sixth World Cup
SVC leads boys, Drasa tops girls at National Schools Age Group Swimming Championships
French Open: Fonseca stuns Djokovic in third-round epic
From school trials to championship success for Lomani Tiko
Valladolid 7s: Fijiana thumped 5-45 by Australia
item.description: The Fiji Airways Fijiana were thumped 45-5 by Australia in their second pool match. The Aussies ran in 7 tries, while Fiji managed to get one back through veteran Reapi Ulunisau.
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji Airways Fijiana were thumped 45-5 by Australia in their second pool match.

The Aussies ran in 7 tries, while Fiji managed to get one back through veteran Reapi Ulunisau.

The Fijiana were outclassed, by the Aussies who seemed to be scoring at will.

The Richie Walker coached side will next play South Africa at 8 tonight.

They had won their first pool game against the USA 26-19.

The Women's cup quarter finals are at 12.49am tomorrow.

The cup semifinals are at 8pm tomorrow, while the Women's cup final kicks off at 1.46am Monday.

In other games, Australia thumped South Africa 26-12, Japan outplayed Brazil 24-7, New Zealand thrashed Argentina 38-7, Canada beat Spain 35-5, while France thrashed Great Britain 34-0.

item.title: Balenawai wins gold after 3 weeks of hard preparation
item.description: Tikiko Balenawai of Namaka Public School claimed gold in the 50 metres backstroke during the National Schools Age Group Championship 2026 at the Damodar Aquatic Centre. The young swimmer says the journey was not easy, as he only had three weeks to prepare for the competition.
item.content:encoded:
Tikiko Balenawai of Namaka Public School claimed gold in the 50 metres backstroke during the National Schools Age Group Championship 2026 at the Damodar Aquatic Centre.

The young swimmer says the journey was not easy, as he only had three weeks to prepare for the competition.

Balenawai says the training sessions were tiring, but his coach, Simba guided him.

He further says he expected to win gold and was determined to give his best.

The Namaka Public student also acknowledged the sacrifices made by his father who is in Australia and thanked his mother for her continuous support.

Balenawai adds that swimming is a good sport because athletes get to enjoy themselves while competing and making new friends.

item.title: Sevu Reece celebrates 100th Crusaders game in style
item.description: Playing 100 games for the Crusaders is no small achievement, especially when you think about the 25 players who reached the milestone before, and now to be among them makes the moment even more special. These were the words of Fiji-born All Blacks winger Sevu Reece after playing his 100th match for the Crusaders against the Hurricanes last night.
item.content:encoded:
Playing 100 games for the Crusaders is no small achievement, especially when you think about the 25 players who reached the milestone before, and now to be among them makes the moment even more special.

These were the words of Fiji-born All Blacks winger Sevu Reece after playing his 100th match for the Crusaders against the Hurricanes last night.

The Kadavu speedster celebrated the milestone in style, scoring a try and kicking the first conversion of his career to help the Crusaders thrash the Hurricanes 47-14.

Reece says it was an amazing moment for him to have the support of his family and village in Christchurch.

The former Queen Victoria School student says having his name on the wall in the gym at Rugby Park alongside the greats for the Crusaders is super special, and he is blessed for the opportunity.

In other results last night, the Queensland Reds beat the Swire Shipping Fijian Drua 45-24.

In today's matches, the Brumbies takes on Moana Pasifika at 4.35pm, the Chiefs face the Blues at 7.05pm while the Force will take on the Warratahs at 9.35pm.

item.title: 22 players named as Fiji prepares for historic World Handball Championship debut
item.description: Handball Fiji has announced its 22-member Women’s Youth National training squad for the upcoming International Handball Federation Women’s Youth (Under-18) World Championship in Romania, where Fiji will make history as the first Pacific island nation to compete on the global stage. Competition for a ticket to Europe remains fierce, with national selectors scheduled to cut the numbers down to a fin
item.content:encoded:
Handball Fiji has announced its 22-member Women’s Youth National training squad for the upcoming International Handball Federation Women’s Youth (Under-18) World Championship in Romania, where Fiji will make history as the first Pacific island nation to compete on the global stage.

Competition for a ticket to Europe remains fierce, with national selectors scheduled to cut the numbers down to a final 16-member travelling team on June 22, 2026.

The side will carry the hopes of the entire region into an elite 32-nation field.

The left wing players named in the 22-member training squad are Ana Biu, Esther Bola, Senitirau Koroinaqara and Koleta Turagakula.

Miliakere Noa, Valerie Raicakacaka and Veniana Tavainavesi have been selected as right wings.

The pivot players in the squad are Elizabeth Colati, Ivamere Buituni, Sefiana Dobui and Bulou Suguta.

Mele Kocovanua and Ro Banuve Logavatu have been named as left backs, while Rositalei Matakarawa, Alena Moto and Litia Vuniwai have been selected as right backs.

The centre back positions have been filled by Adi Sainimili Logavatu, Mereani Moto and Paulina Raogo.

The goalkeepers in the squad are Paulina Nagata, Lavenia Ravuna and Helena Tungi.

Fiji has been handed a formidable challenge after being drawn into Group A alongside global handball powerhouses Croatia, France, and Egypt.

The World Championship features the world's finest rising talent including defending champions Spain.

Fiji and Mexico are both making their tournament debuts.

Head of Delegation Lusi Rokoura praised the grit of the 22 players.

She says our young, fast-improving squad is stepping onto the world stage proud, determined, and ready.

However she says their journey is about much more than handball.

Rokoura stresses this is about youth empowerment, breaking barriers for women in sports, and flying the Fiji flag on the global stage.

She is also calling on corporate Fiji, sporting stakeholders, and the Government to stand firmly behind these incredible young women.

The Head of Delegation says under official campaign banner, "Rising From the Islands to the World Stage," their journey proves that young athletes have the talent and drive to step out of our island geography and compete with the best in the world.

Rokoura adds this historic campaign will lay the foundation for Brisbane 2032, building the high-performance pipeline and elite experience necessary to carry Fiji's flag to the Olympic stage. The World Championship is from July 29th to August 9th 2026.

Fiji is pooled with Croatia, France and Egypt in Group A.

They will face France at 11pm on 29th July, Croatia at 9.45pm on 30th July, and Egypt at 9.45pm on 1st August.
